summary: Closed, unmerged PR that attempted to match `src/sysdolphin/baselib/spline.c`. The diff changed storage-class/inline qualifiers for spline helper functions, extracted a tiny `spl_GetArcLengthDx` helper in `splArcLengthGetParameter`, removed the now-static B-spline helper from the public header, renamed spline `.sdata2` symbols back to compiler-style `@N` labels, and flipped `spline.c` from `NonMatching` to `Matching` in `configure.py`. The automated decomp.dev report showed `splArcLengthGetParameter` and `.sdata2` reaching 100%, with +912 matched code bytes and +56 matched data bytes.

key_files.role: Primary decomp target; adjusted helper visibility/inline qualifiers and `splArcLengthGetParameter` code shape.
Header cleanup; removed `splGetBSplinePoint` declaration after making it file-local static.
Renamed spline `.sdata2` constants from `HSD_Spline_804DE620`-style names to compiler-style `@13`, `@14`, etc.

what_changed: `splGetCardinalPoint` and `splGetBezierPoint` definitions were changed from plain `void` functions to `inline void`.
`splGetBSplinePoint` was changed from a non-static function to `static void`, and its prototype was removed from `spline.h`.
`splArcLengthPolynomial` was changed from `inline f32` to `static f32`.
A new `static f32 spl_GetArcLengthDx(f32 start, f32 midpoint)` helper was added, returning `(midpoint - start) / 8.0F`.

`splArcLengthGetParameter` was reshaped: `result` was declared after `start`/`end`, the local `midpoint` const was removed, `result` became the midpoint value, and `dx`/`middle` were declared separately before use.
The Simpson integration loop now calls `spl_GetArcLengthDx(start, result)` and uses `result` instead of `midpoint` in polynomial evaluation and bisection updates.

`config/GALE01/symbols.txt` replaced twelve `HSD_Spline_804DE620` through `HSD_Spline_804DE654` `.sdata2` labels with numbered labels `@13`, `@14`, `@15`, `@17`, `@18`, `@19`, `@80`, `@82`, `@91`, `@101`, `@178`, and `@179`.
Automated report for GALE01 showed two new matches: `splArcLengthGetParameter` improved from 98.77% to 100.00% (+12 bytes), and spline `.sdata2` improved from 85.71% to 100.00% (+8 bytes).
smart_moves: Used minimal storage-class and inline qualifier changes instead of rewriting spline math, keeping behavior stable while altering emitted code enough to match.
Made `splGetBSplinePoint` truly file-local and removed its header declaration, aligning source visibility with the new `static` definition.
Extracted the `(midpoint - start) / 8.0F` expression into a one-line static helper, a small codegen nudge that affected `splArcLengthGetParameter` without changing the algorithm.
Reused `result` as the midpoint variable throughout the binary-search loop, matching the apparent original code shape more closely than a separate `midpoint` const.
Adjusted `.sdata2` symbol names to compiler-style anonymous labels, allowing the data section to match rather than preserving address-derived semantic names.
decomp_lessons: For MWCC-style matching, `inline` versus `static` versus external linkage can be enough to change function emission and object matching.
If a helper is only used within one C file, making it `static` and removing its header prototype can be necessary for both symbol visibility and matching.
Tiny helper functions can be useful matching shims: extracting a simple arithmetic expression can influence register allocation, call structure, or scheduling in a tight loop.
Local declaration order and whether a value is represented by a named variable such as `result` versus a `const midpoint` can matter for matching.
Do not force semantic names on literal-table symbols when the matching symbol map expects compiler-generated `@N` labels.
After a unit reaches object match, update `configure.py` from `NonMatching` to `Matching`, but verify whether the PR actually merged before treating it as landed.
naming_conventions: Public spline APIs in this slice use the `splGet*` prefix, such as `splGetHelmite`, `splGetCardinalPoint`, `splGetBezierPoint`, and `splGetSplinePoint`.
Private/internal helpers in this file use a `spl_` prefix with an underscore, as seen with added `spl_GetArcLengthDx` and existing names like `spl_IterateSimpsonsMiddle` and `spl_GetCoeffs`.
The public type name remains `HSD_Spline` in `spline.h`.
Anonymous `.sdata2` literals may be named with `@N` labels in `symbols.txt`; this PR moved away from `HSD_Spline_<address>` names for the affected constants.
Header declarations should track linkage: `splGetBSplinePoint` was removed from `spline.h` once it became `static`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Toggle helper functions between `inline`, `static`, and external definitions to control whether code is emitted, inlined, or externally visible.
Introduce a small static helper for repeated arithmetic when the original codegen appears to require a separate computation boundary.
Reshape local variables in a loop by replacing a temporary `midpoint` with the function result variable and separating `dx`/`middle` declarations.
Use the same midpoint variable in endpoint polynomial calls and bisection assignments to preserve expression identity in generated code.
Clean up `.sdata2` labels in `symbols.txt` when literal naming affects data matching.
Only mark an object `Matching` in `configure.py` once the diff and automated report support the match.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice.

The only comment was the decomp.dev automated GALE01 report, showing matched code 61.12% (+0.02%, +912 bytes), linked code 33.65% (+0.06%, +2268 bytes), matched data 37.78% (+56 bytes), and linked data 32.29% (+56 bytes).
The PR was closed with `merged_at` null, so future reuse should verify whether this exact change landed in another PR or branch before assuming it is in master.
follow_up_queries: Search later history for `spl_GetArcLengthDx` to see whether this closed-unmerged PR was superseded or landed elsewhere.
Check current `src/sysdolphin/baselib/spline.c` qualifiers for `splGetCardinalPoint`, `splGetBSplinePoint`, `splGetBezierPoint`, and `splArcLengthPolynomial` before reusing this tactic.
Look for later PRs touching `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` around `.sdata2:0x804DE620` through `.sdata2:0x804DE654`.
Compare current `configure.py` object status for `sysdolphin/baselib/spline.c` against this PR's `Matching` flip.
